entries.learning_activity: (b) Discuss colonial economic systems in Africa (Establishment, characteristics, sectors, labour issues)

entries.assessment: The colonial economic systems in Africa are accurately discussed

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of colonial systems in Africa
entries.learning_activity: (c) Assess the nature and patterns of colonial social services in Africa
teaching_learning_activities: Facilitate students in four groups and assign each group one of the colonial social services (education, health, water, housing) to explore and write a report on the nature and patterns of such a service. Let them share the report with the whole class for discussions
entries.assessment: The nature and patterns of colonial social services in Africa are accurately assessed
resources: On-line sources, pictures and photographs on the nature and patterns of colonial social services in Africa
entries.references: TIE, History for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of colonial systems in Africa
entries.learning_activity: (d) Relate colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa with the current African systems
teaching_learning_activities: Guide students to research on the relationship between the colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa with the current African systems (focusing on similarities and differences)
entries.assessment: The colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa are comprehensively related with the current African systems
resources: On-line sources on relationships between colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa with the current African systems
entries.references: TIE, History for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the history of nationalist movements in Africa
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe nationalist movements in Africa
teaching_learning_activities: By using jigsaw, guide students to describe the nationalist movements in Africa, emphasising tracing the development of pre-nationalist movements (religious, peasants, welfare and workers` associations). Guide them to relate pre-nationalist movements with mass movements
entries.assessment: Nationalist movements in Africa are correctly described
resources: Photographs and on-line sources on nationalist movements in Africa
entries.references: TIE, History for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the history of nationalist movements in Africa
entries.learning_activity: (b) Assess the rise of nationalist movements in Africa
teaching_learning_activities: By using group discussion, guide students to assess the internal and external forces for the emergence of nationalist movements in Africa

entries.references: TIE, History for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the history of nationalist movements in Africa
entries.learning_activity: (c) Appraise the lessons drawn from the nationalist movements in Africa
teaching_learning_activities: In manageable groups, let students reflect on lessons that the current African countries can learn from nationalist movements
entries.assessment: The lesson drawn from the nationalist movements are appropriately reviewed
